eventDetails: A 24-hour product-building hackathon where teams solve real campus and community problems using web, AI, automation, or IoT. Participants will pitch their prototype to mentors and judges.
A beginner-friendly session on machine learning workflows, prompt engineering, AI tools, and how students can start building practical AI projects without getting lost in theory.
A hands-on workshop focused on building REST APIs, connecting frontend forms, validating data, and preparing a project for backend integration with SQLite.
A capture-the-flag challenge covering web security, cryptography basics, forensics, and logical puzzles. Designed for students who want a practical entry into cybersecurity.
A guided lab where students explore sensors, microcontrollers, data collection, and small automation prototypes that can be extended into real club projects.
